A vulnerability scan passively identifies potential flaws, whereas a penetration test actively exploits those flaws to prove business impact
A vulnerability scan is only performed by external auditors, whereas a penetration test is only performed by internal IT
A vulnerability scan is strictly quantitative, whereas a penetration test is strictly qualitative

questions.question: To effectively mitigate a Session Fixation attack, what action MUST the web application take immediately after a user successfully authenticates?
options: Store the user's password in a Base64 encoded cookie
Invalidate the pre-login Session ID and issue a completely new, cryptographically secure Session ID
Extend the Absolute Timeout limit to 24 hours to ensure continuous connectivity
Remove the SameSite attribute from the session cookie

questions.question: Which of the following is widely recognized as the industry standard for secure, hardened configuration guidelines for operating systems, cloud providers, and network devices?

The MITRE ATT&CK Framework
The CIS (Center for Internet Security) Benchmarks
The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)

By utilizing machine learning to establish a baseline of normal activity for each user and triggering alerts when significant deviations occur
By blocking all incoming traffic from foreign IP addresses automatically
By enforcing Multi-Factor Authentication for all administrative remote access

To ensure that security is 'Shifted Left', automatically halting the deployment if critical vulnerabilities are detected before the code reaches production
To translate the application's source code into Python for faster execution
To encrypt the application's database at rest using AES-256

options: Disabling ICMP echo requests (ping)
Enabling Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR)
Enabling TCP SYN cookies (net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies = 1)
Disabling IPv6 auto-configuration

Because advanced attackers may have installed stealthy rootkits or deep persistence mechanisms that evade detection during eradication
Because restoring from backups is the only way to generate a proper forensic hash
Because the SIEM platform cannot monitor servers that have been previously infected

Deploying a strict Content Security Policy (CSP) in the web browser

Trust is granted implicitly if the device is connected to the corporate VPN
Trust is established via a single initial authentication that remains valid for 30 days

Utilizing Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and configuration management tools to automatically enforce and revert systems to codified baselines
Requiring a 72-hour reporting window for all administrative registry changes

questions.question: What is the primary operational distinction between a standard Penetration Test and a Red Team engagement?
options: A penetration test includes physical security breaches, whereas a Red Team focuses solely on web applications
A Red Team is a stealthy, objective-based adversary simulation designed specifically to test the detection and response capabilities of the SOC
A Red Team focuses on finding as many vulnerabilities as possible, whereas a penetration test focuses on exploiting a single zero-day

SNMP (Simple Network Management Protocol)
SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol)
FTP (File Transfer Protocol)

Mandatory Access Control (MAC)

Device Manager
Event Log

The attacker can intercept plaintext credentials in transit

Rolling back the server's firmware to the previous stable version
Implementing Virtual Patching by configuring the Web Application Firewall (WAF) to drop packets containing the exploit payload

It translates the SIEM alerts into regulatory compliance reports for HIPAA or GDPR
It provides a standardized taxonomy to track specific adversary behaviors, allowing the SOC to identify systemic defensive weaknesses over time

questions.question: Under the GDPR, what is the maximum allowable timeframe an organization has to notify the supervisory authority after discovering a personal data breach?
options: 24 hours
72 hours
10 business days
30 calendar days

questions.question: To effectively defend against offline brute-forcing and pre-computed Rainbow Table attacks, what must be applied to a user's password before it is hashed and stored in a database?
options: A digital signature generated by the Root Certificate Authority
A cryptographic 'salt' combined with a high-iteration Key Derivation Function (KDF)
Full Disk Encryption (FDE) using AES-GCM
An X.509 digital certificate bound to the user's IP address

options: Read a protected file without the appropriate read permissions
Execute a file temporarily acquiring the permissions of the file's owner
Delete files that are marked as undeletable by root
Change the file's MAC address attributes

questions.question: According to the Order of Volatility, why is it a critical mistake to immediately reboot a compromised server upon discovering a breach?
options: Rebooting the server will automatically send an alert to the attacker's C2 infrastructure
Rebooting will permanently destroy highly volatile forensic evidence residing in the system's RAM, such as active network connections and decrypted payloads
Rebooting bypasses the BIOS/UEFI password, granting the attacker permanent access
Rebooting clears the SIEM correlation rules, causing a flood of false positive alerts

To integrate disparate security tools via APIs and automate incident triage and containment playbooks, drastically reducing response times
To serve as a passive network tap for capturing cleartext TLS traffic
To act as the primary, immutable storage repository for long-term log retention

Static Application Security Testing (SAST)

Configuration Management Database (CMDB)

questions.question: To mitigate the risk of leaked credentials in the cloud, what architectural mechanism should be used to provide applications (like an EC2 instance) with temporary, auto-rotating access to cloud APIs instead of using static, hardcoded access keys?
options: Network Access Control Lists (NACLs)
Bring Your Own Key (BYOK)
IAM Assumed Roles via the Security Token Service (STS)

SMS-based One-Time Passwords
FIDO2 / WebAuthn utilizing hardware security keys

options: SMB/networking stack
Device drivers
Win32 API
HAL

questions.question: During post-exploitation, an attacker extracts a user's NTLM hash from the memory of a compromised workstation and uses it to authenticate to an adjacent file server without ever knowing the user's plaintext password. What is this technique known as?
options: Token Impersonation
Pass-the-Hash (PtH)
Unquoted Service Path exploitation
Buffer Overflow

questions.question: Which of the following represents the most effective programmatic defense against SQL Injection (SQLi) vulnerabilities in web applications?

Utilizing Parameterized Queries (Prepared Statements) to force the database to treat input as literal values

WPA-Enterprise forces individual user authentication against a RADIUS server, rather than relying on a shared password

options: It cannot provide non-repudiation or authenticity
It is computationally expensive and significantly slower than symmetric encryption
It relies on the AES-256 algorithm, which is highly vulnerable to quantum computing
It requires both communicating parties to securely share the exact same private key beforehand

questions.question: How do Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) phishing attacks successfully bypass traditional Time-based One-Time Password (TOTP) MFA solutions?

By utilizing a malicious reverse proxy to intercept and steal the authenticated session cookie after the user inputs their MFA token
By exploiting a vulnerability in the RADIUS authentication protocol
By sending millions of push notifications until the user accidentally approves the request

questions.question: According to the principle of Fail-Safe Defaults, how should a Web Application Firewall (WAF) be configured to respond if its primary inspection engine crashes due to a software bug?

SNMPv3 utilizes a decentralized MIB architecture to prevent single points of failure
